reasoning: The question asks whether an H5 virus will receive an IRAT emergence risk rating of "high" (score >= 8.0) before January 1, 2026. Historically, no virus has ever scored an 8.0 or above on the emergence criteria (the highest being 7.5 for an H1N1 strain). Recent H5N1 assessments by the CDC in 2024 and 2025 (e.g., May 2025 updates) have consistently placed the risk in the "moderate" category (scores ~4.0 to ~5.8). Reaching an 8.0 would require a literal pandemic onset, characterized by sustained and highly efficient human-to-human transmission, which is not currently occurring, nor has there been any breaking news to suggest a sudden, catastrophic shift as of early November 2025. Given the extreme rarity of this threshold, the absence of corresponding alarming news, and the very low market baseline (2% on Oct 30, 2025), I predict a 2% chance of this occurring by the end of 2025 (in the remaining ~1.5 months).
